name: WeChat Editorial Article
description: A polished long-form visual article for WeChat-style reading, with editorial pacing and refined typography.
usage: Use for WeChat official-account articles, brand essays, thought pieces, release notes, interviews, and long visual reading.
best_for: Narrative articles, thought pieces, announcements, interviews, and polished brand essays.
Briefs where readability, paragraph rhythm, quotes, and section transitions matter most.
avoid_for: Single-frame campaign posters that need one dominant visual hit.
Highly interactive app or dashboard interfaces.
layout_rules: Use a centered article column with a 62-68 ch text measure and occasional full-bleed visual bands.
Start with title, deck, metadata, and a restrained editorial lead-in before body sections.
Use pull quotes, chapter dividers, side notes, and image captions to create magazine rhythm.
Avoid nested cards; article sections should feel like editorial layout, not a dashboard.
style_rules: Prefer Noto Serif SC for titles or quotes and Noto Sans SC/system UI for body copy.
Use near-black text, warm off-white paper, subtle gray borders, and one restrained brand color.
Keep spacing generous and mature; let typography carry the polish more than decoration.
Use thin rules, small labels, and quiet callouts for refinement.
content_rules: Preserve the user's final copy order unless rewriting is requested.
Use concise headings that advance the argument.
Treat captions and quotes as narrative devices.
End with a deliberate CTA, note, or closing thought.
quality_checks: The page should be comfortable to read from top to bottom.
Headings, body, quotes, captions, and CTA must have distinct typographic roles.
The result should not resemble a SaaS landing page or a dashboard.
